title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The digital revolution has (0) about dramatic changes in how we live and work. In just a few decades, technology has transformed almost every (1) of modern life. The internet, in particular, has had a profound (2) on communication, allowing people to connect instantly across the globe. Social media platforms have changed the way we (3) relationships and share information. Meanwhile, smartphones have put powerful computers in our pockets, giving us (4) to endless information and services. Businesses have had to (5) to this new digital landscape or risk being left behind. Many traditional industries have been disrupted by innovative digital companies. However, the digital revolution has also (6) concerns about privacy, security, and the spread of misinformation. As technology continues to (7) at a rapid pace, society must find ways to maximize its benefits while minimizing potential (8).

example.explanation: 'Brought about' is a phrasal verb that means to cause or cause change.

questions.explanation: 'Brought about' is a phrasal verb that means to cause or cause change.
'Have an impact on' is the standard expression to describe a significant effect.
'Maintain relationships' is the appropriate collocation to talk about maintaining relationships.
'Access to' is the correct expression to refer to the possibility of obtaining or using something.
'Adapt to' is the correct verb to describe the action of adjusting to new circumstances.
'Raised concerns' is the correct collocation meaning to raise or generate concerns.
'Evolve' is the most appropriate verb to describe the continuous development of technology.
'Harms' is the correct noun in this context to refer to potential harm or harm.
